goal: Generate a 4:3 nature experience image of Wekiwa Springs State Park — crystal-clear 68°F spring pool with turquoise-blue water, ancient live oaks with Spanish moss creating a cathedral canopy, two orange kayaks on the bank, family sedan barely visible in background parking — no people, must communicate 'hidden Florida natural wonder only accessible by car'.

creative_direction.core_idea: Wekiwa Springs — 35 minutos desde MCO en carro, el secreto de Florida que los turistas LATAM no saben
creative_direction.visual_thesis: Esta imagen crea deseo: agua cristalina + naturaleza antigua + solo accesible en carro

composition.shot_type: environmental landscape — spring pool as hero, canopy as frame
composition.layout: Spring pool fills the lower 50% of frame with crystal-clear turquoise water; ancient oak canopy with Spanish moss forms a natural cathedral ceiling in the upper 50%; two orange kayaks anchored on the left bank; family sedan barely discernible in background trees

camera.framing: 4:3 horizontal — wide to capture canopy cathedral + spring pool in one frame
camera.angle: slightly elevated, looking toward the spring head — shows both water clarity and canopy depth
camera.lens_style: 24mm wide — captures the cathedral canopy scale and spring pool together
camera.depth_of_field: wide — spring water clarity and canopy both sharp; sedan in background softly visible

overlay_compatibility.text_safe_zone: upper right — dark canopy area with filtered light rays
overlay_compatibility.logo_safe_zone: lower right corner — water surface or sand bank

subject.type: landscape — spring pool as primary subject
subject.primary: Wekiwa Springs crystal-clear spring pool — turquoise-blue water so transparent the white sandy bottom is visible at depth
subject.secondary: Ancient live oak trees — massive trunks, centuries old, Spanish moss hanging in long curtains creating a natural cathedral
subject.props: two orange kayaks pulled up on the sandy bank — the only human-made objects in the foreground
subject.scale_element: family sedan — barely visible through the trees in the background parking lot, soft focus, just recognizable as a car
critical_attributes: water must be impossibly clear — white sandy bottom visible through the turquoise
Spanish moss on oak trees is essential — it identifies Florida spring specifically
two orange kayaks on bank — warm accent color against blue water and green
sedan in background barely visible — suggests 'they drove here'

scene.location: Wekiwa Springs State Park, Apopka, Orange County, FL — 35 minutes from MCO airport
scene.time_of_day: mid-morning — dappled light through canopy, some light rays visible, not harsh midday
environment: crystal-clear freshwater spring pool — constant 68°F, turquoise-blue, white sandy bottom
ancient live oak trees — massive gnarled trunks, wide canopy spread
Spanish moss hanging from branches — long gray-green curtains
sandy spring bank — white sand at water's edge
no commercial facilities visible — state park natural context
filtered sunlight through canopy creates dappled light on water surface
scene.background_view: Deep canopy of live oaks and subtropical trees — dark green with filtered light; sedan barely visible as soft suggestion
critical_scene_rules: No people — no swimmers, no kayakers, no visitors
Water clarity is mandatory — white sandy bottom must be visible through turquoise water
Spanish moss must be present — it is the Florida spring identity marker
Two orange kayaks must be present on bank
No commercial facilities, no signage visible
Natural state park context only — no manicured resort aesthetic

lighting.type: mid-morning dappled forest light — sunbeams through oak canopy
effects: dappled light on water surface — creates moving light patterns on turquoise water
some light rays visible through Spanish moss — cathedral light effect
water appears lit from within — crystal clarity makes it glow turquoise
oak trunks in shadow create depth and mystery in the canopy
orange kayaks pop as warm accent against cool blue-green environment

dominant: warm gray live oak bark and Spanish moss
accent: orange kayaks — #FF6B35 range, warm pop against cool environment
white sandy bottom visible through water
soft warm light rays through canopy
rules: Orange kayaks are the only warm accent — they must pop against the cool blue-green environment
Water must be distinctly turquoise — not gray, not muddy, not dark — crystal clear is non-negotiable
Canopy creates deep green shade — the contrast with the lit spring pool is the visual engine
Spanish moss adds gray-green texture that identifies this as Florida, not generic forest

negative_prompt: no dark or murky water — must be crystal clear
no flat green water — must be distinctly turquoise
no pine trees — live oaks with Spanish moss only
master_prompt: Create a 4:3 nature experience image at 2000x1500 pixels for a car rental marketplace showing Wekiwa Springs State Park in Apopka, Florida — 35 minutes from MCO airport. The crystal-clear freshwater spring pool dominates the lower half of the frame: turquoise-blue water so transparent the white sandy bottom is fully visible at depth, as if lit from within. The upper half shows a natural cathedral of ancient live oak trees — massive gnarled trunks with centuries of growth, long curtains of Spanish moss hanging from the branches, filtering mid-morning sunlight into dappled rays that dance on the water surface. Two orange kayaks are pulled up on the white sandy bank to the left — the only human-made objects in the foreground. A family sedan is barely discernible through the trees in the background — soft focus, just visible enough to suggest 'someone drove here.' Shot with a 24mm wide lens at slightly elevated angle to capture both the spring pool clarity and the full canopy cathedral in one frame. No people, no swimmers, no commercial facilities. The color palette is cool and natural: turquoise-blue water, deep forest green canopy, gray-green Spanish moss, warm orange kayaks as the only accent. Dappled light creates movement on the water surface. This image must communicate an irresistible hidden Florida secret — natural wonder that only road-trippers with a rental car can discover. Avoid people, murky water, dark water, pine trees, commercial signage, resort context, theme park context, ocean, mountains, and any text or watermarks.
